headline: Is Best Buy (BBY) Pricing Reflect The DCF Upside Or P/E Premium Risk
summary: If you are wondering whether Best Buy is attractively priced or if the stock is still adjusting to changing retail trends, this article will walk you through how the current share price compares with different ways of thinking about value. Best Buy last closed at US$65.95, with returns of 5.1% over the past 7 days, 1.3% over 30 days, and year-to-date and 1-year returns of 4.7% and 7.6% declines respectively, alongside a 22.8% decline over 5 years. Recent headlines around Best Buy have...

headline: Iran war pressures consumer costs: What does it mean for retailers?
summary: Target (TGT) narrowly beat earnings expectations but posted another quarter of declining sales, leaving investors focused on the company's upcoming analyst day and long-term turnaround plans. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma joins Opening Bid host Brian Sozzi to explain how geopolitical tensions, higher gas prices, and other macro uncertainties are impacting consumer behavior and what that means for Target, Best Buy (BBY), and other retailers. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Opening Bid

headline: Best Buy (BBY) Is Up 5.1% After Stronger Margins Offset Soft Sales In Q4 Results
summary: Best Buy Co., Inc. recently reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2026 results, with US$13,814 million in quarterly sales, US$541 million in quarterly net income, full-year revenue of US$41.69 billion, and issued fiscal 2027 guidance for revenue between US$41.2 billion and US$42.1 billion alongside a quarterly dividend increase to US$0.96 per share. Profitability improved meaningfully even as comparable sales slipped, helped by higher-margin areas such as Best Buy Ads and the expanding U.S...

headline: Wall Street Bleeds as Iran Shuts the Hormuz Tap
summary: Markets staggered through a bruising Tuesday as Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz sent oil prices surging and reignited inflation fears. Only a late-session promise from President Trump pulled the major averages back from the edge, but the damage was already done.

raw.headline: Wall Street Bleeds as Iran Shuts the Hormuz Tap
raw.summary: Markets staggered through a bruising Tuesday as Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz sent oil prices surging and reignited inflation fears. Only a late-session promise from President Trump pulled the major averages back from the edge, but the damage was already done.

headline: Best Buy Earnings Beat Highlights Shift To Higher Margin Digital Model
summary: Best Buy (NYSE:BBY) reported stronger than expected Q4 earnings, supported by growth in its Ads and digital marketplace businesses. The company highlighted new small format stores and AI powered customer experiences as key areas of focus. Management raised the dividend, pointing to an emphasis on cash returns to shareholders alongside its evolving retail model. Best Buy enters this next phase with its share price at $65.95 and a mixed return profile. The stock is up 5.1% over the past week...
